hook.text: A foreign key says every order must have a valid customer. Consistency means this rule holds after every transaction.

buildup.text: Consistency guarantees the database moves from one valid state to another. Constraints are never violated.

discovery.text: CHECK constraints, NOT NULL, UNIQUE, and foreign keys all define what 'valid state' means.

twist.text: Within a transaction, the database may temporarily violate constraints. It checks them at COMMIT time.

climax.text: Deferred constraints (Postgres) wait until COMMIT to validate. Useful for circular foreign keys.

punchline.text: Consistency: every committed transaction leaves the database in a valid state.

quiz.question: When are deferred constraints checked?
options: At COMMIT time, not when the individual statement executes
Only when the database is restarted
